executive_summary: Your Ad-Tech company has a strong foundation for AI adoption — medium data maturity, existing API integrations, and a clear pain point in manual reporting. The highest-ROI first project is automated weekly campaign report generation: it solves an immediate, quantified problem (4-6 hours/AM/week), requires no new data infrastructure, and delivers visible value to clients within 3 weeks. With GDPR compliance, we recommend Anthropic's API with a signed DPA over OpenAI.
strategic_assessment: You are in a good position to start AI. You have usable data, real pain points, and a growth budget. The absence of ML engineers means we stay with managed API approaches — no fine-tuning, no self-hosted models. Your GDPR requirement is manageable with Anthropic's DPA. The 90-day pilot timeline is realistic for the recommended first project.

use_cases.title: Automated Weekly Campaign Report Generation
use_cases.description: Build a Claude-powered report generator that queries your campaign APIs (Google Ads, Meta), pulls last week's performance data, and drafts a structured client-ready report in your company's format. Account managers review and send — no more starting from scratch.
use_cases.business_value: Saves 4-6 hours per account manager per week. At 20 AMs, that's 80-120 hours/week recovered — equivalent to 2-3 FTEs. Clients receive consistent, faster reporting.
use_cases.data_requirements: Campaign performance data from Google Ads API and Meta Ads API. You already have this — it just needs aggregation.
use_cases.team_requirements: 1 backend engineer (Python). No ML expertise required.
key_risks: AI-generated insights may miss nuance that an experienced AM would catch
Clients may be unhappy if report quality drops vs. human-crafted versions
mitigation: AM review step before sending — AI drafts, human approves
Start with internal reports only, move to client-facing after 2-week internal pilot

use_cases.title: Campaign Q&A Agent for Account Managers
use_cases.description: A natural language interface over campaign data that lets AMs ask questions like 'Why did CTR drop last week?' or 'Which creatives are outperforming?' — and get instant, data-grounded answers. Reduces client response latency from hours to seconds.
use_cases.business_value: Eliminates ad-hoc data analysis time (~2 hours/AM/week). Enables real-time client Q&A during calls. Competitive differentiator for client retention.
use_cases.data_requirements: Structured campaign metrics database. Medium data maturity means some data normalisation needed first.
use_cases.team_requirements: 1-2 backend engineers. Some prompt engineering skill needed.
key_risks: Hallucinated metrics in a client-facing context would be damaging
Keeping data fresh requires robust pipeline
mitigation: Ground all answers in retrieved data — never let model fabricate numbers
Display data sources alongside answers so AMs can verify

use_cases.title: New Account Manager Onboarding Knowledge Base
use_cases.description: A RAG system over your internal playbooks, campaign case studies, and client history that new AMs can query to get up to speed. Reduces time-to-productivity from 3 months to 6 weeks.
use_cases.business_value: 6 weeks of productivity recovered per new hire. At 8 hires per year, that's 48 weeks of additional AM capacity. Reduced senior AM mentoring load.
use_cases.data_requirements: Internal documents: playbooks, case studies, SOPs, client briefs. Must be digitised and centralised.
use_cases.team_requirements: 1 backend engineer. Significant content curation effort from senior AMs.
key_risks: Document quality is inconsistent — garbage in, garbage out
New AMs may over-rely on AI and miss nuance
mitigation: Content audit sprint before building RAG — only index high-quality docs
Frame as 'first draft answer' tool, not authoritative source

recommended_first_project: Automated Weekly Campaign Report Generation — lowest risk, fastest ROI, zero new infrastructure needed. Ship in 3 weeks and prove AI value before committing to more complex projects.

deliverables: Audit Google Ads and Meta Ads API access
Define report template with 3 representative AMs
Set up Anthropic API account with GDPR DPA signed
Build data aggregation script for campaign metrics
roadmap_90_day.success_criteria: Data pipeline runs end-to-end, pulling last 7 days of campaign data for 3 test accounts

deliverables: Create AM review interface (simple web form or Slack bot)
Run 2-week internal pilot with 3 AMs on their own accounts
Collect AM feedback and iterate on prompt/format
roadmap_90_day.success_criteria: 3 AMs generating draft reports in <5 minutes. AM satisfaction score >7/10. Zero reports sent to clients without AM review.

deliverables: Roll out to all 20 account managers
Begin scoping Campaign Q&A Agent based on learnings
roadmap_90_day.success_criteria: 80%+ of AMs using the tool weekly. Measurable reduction in reporting time. Zero client complaints about report quality.

deliverables: ROI report: time saved, AM satisfaction, client feedback
Prioritise next use case (Campaign Q&A Agent or Onboarding KB)
Present results to leadership with funding ask for Phase 2
roadmap_90_day.success_criteria: Documented ROI case. Leadership approval to proceed to Phase 2 use case.

quick_wins: Use Claude API to generate one-paragraph campaign summaries for your top 5 clients this week — no infrastructure needed, just a script
Ask AMs to document their top 10 most-asked client questions — this seeds the Q&A agent backlog
Sign Anthropic GDPR DPA in week 1 — unblocks everything, takes 1 hour
things_to_avoid: Don't start with the Campaign Q&A Agent — it's more complex and you need to prove AI value first with something simpler
Don't use OpenAI API — you have GDPR requirements and need a DPA. Anthropic provides this.
Don't skip the AM review step on reports — one bad AI-generated insight sent to a client can damage the relationship
Don't try to build all 3 use cases at once — you'll ship none of them properly
questions_to_answer_first: Do you have API access to Google Ads and Meta Ads, or are you pulling data manually?
Who is the internal champion for this project — an AM lead or a technical lead?
What does a 'good' weekly report look like? Can you show us 3 examples of reports your best AMs produce?
How are reports currently delivered — email, PDF, in the client portal?
What's the GDPR data controller/processor relationship with your clients — do they need to be notified that AI touches their data?
readiness_blockers: GDPR DPA with Anthropic not yet signed
No ML engineers on team — limits to managed API approaches only
Data exists but is not yet aggregated in a single place for AI consumption
readiness_accelerators: Medium data maturity — data exists and is mostly accessible
Existing API integrations with ad platforms — no data extraction problem
Growth budget — can afford API costs and engineering time
Clear, quantified pain points — easy to measure ROI
Basic AI experience — team understands APIs and is not starting from zero
